elements.content: Comic Flow Image Demo: Multi-Page Regression
This regression fixture validates flow-image behavior in normal document flow (Top-and-Bottom style): images should move with text and paginate deterministically across multiple pages.
Panel 1: The Setup - What Could Possibly Go Wrong?

The brilliant, if slightly misguided, Professor Proton prepares to test his latest invention. What is he hoping to achieve? Even he is not entirely sure. He checks his notes, circles three contradictory hypotheses, and confidently declares all of them correct at once.

Witnesses report that he adjusted every dial to "maximum curiosity" and then muttered something about scientific destiny. The lab lights flicker, coffee vibrates ominously, and a nearby whiteboard acquires several equations that nobody remembers writing.
Panel 2: The Action - A Nerd-Splosion of Epic Proportions!

With a confident press of the button, the Hyper-Nerd-inator springs to life, unleashing a chaotic but oddly charming explosion of all things nerdy. Graph paper erupts from vents. Action figures achieve temporary orbit. A dramatic choir appears to sing in binary.

In the middle of the turbulence, Professor Proton remains calm and records observations with impressive precision: "loud noises, unusual sparkle density, and approximately twelve percent more dramatic cape physics than predicted."

The smoke clears to reveal the professor's unexpected transformation. He may not be the hero the world needs, but he is the hero it deserves. Armed with encyclopedic trivia, unstoppable enthusiasm, and suspiciously aerodynamic glasses, he vows to use his powers responsibly.

Post-incident report for pagination stress testing: Flow images remain in sequence with surrounding text, and this sentence intentionally repeats to enforce multi-page continuation behavior.
